title: Geist (Moff)
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] character|the [[Unidentified Mon Calamari cruiser (Geist)|Mon Calamari cruiser]] by [[Geist (cruiser)|the same name]]|linebreak=1}} {{FAreview}} {{Character |type=Sith Empire |image=[[File:Geist.jpg]] |name=Geist |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[138 ABY]], [[Taivas]][[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]] |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]][[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]]{{Fact}} |pronouns=He/him{{Fact}} |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Black]][[Legacy (2006) 15|''Legacy'' (2006) 15]] |feathers= |eyes=Golden |skin=Dark |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Fel Empire]] **[[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]] **[[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]][[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]] *[[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] **[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] Council **Imperial [[Army/Legends|Army]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Thus we are all sent back to our kennels.|Geist bemoans the Moff Council's treatment by the Sith|}} '''Geist''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as both a [[High Moff]] and the [[General/Legends|general]] in [[Commander/Legends|command]] of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] in the [[Fel Empire]] and later in [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]]. Preferring war over peace, Geist led his [[Troop/Legends|troops]] from the front lines and favored the use of threats and intimidation as tactics. As a member of the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]] in [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]'s Empire, Geist accepted an alliance with the [[One Sith]] during the [[Sith–Imperial War]] between the Empire and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]], but the Empire's ultimate [[Battle of Caamas|victory]] in the war in [[130 ABY]] saw the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]] [[Attack on Emperor Fel|usurp]] the Imperial throne and send Fel fleeing into [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]]. Geist and the rest of the Moff Council swore allegiance to the new [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] and served in his new [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] Empire. Over the next seven [[Standard year/Legends|years]], Geist came to lament the Moffs' lack of real power under Sith rule, and he declared as much during a meeting of the council on the Empire's [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] in [[137 ABY]]. The Empire was fighting the [[Second Imperial Civil War]] against Fel's [[Empire-in-exile]], the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]], and the [[New Jedi Order]], and late in the year, Geist was given command of a [[Imperial Navy/Legends|naval]] mission to confront the Empire's foes. The Sith had recently [[Final Protocol|poisoned]] the planet [[Dac/Legends|Dac]], and Geist [[Space warfare/Legends|led]] the [[Outer Rim Third Fleet]] against the [[Core Fleet|Alliance Remnant forces]] that sought to [[Evacuation of Dac|evacuate]] its citizens. However, a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] loyal to Fel arrived to reinforce the Remnant, and Geist was able to convince his stubborn Sith overseer [[Darth Stryfe]] that a retreat was preferable to destruction. [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Krayt began a significant military push against his enemies in [[138 ABY]], and when he learned that the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] had a [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]] on the planet [[Taivas]], he placed Geist in command of the [[Ground warfare/Legends|ground forces]] that would participate in the [[Attack on the Hidden Temple|attack]]. Geist led [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], [[Walker/Legends|walkers]], [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and Sith [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]] against the Jedi fortress after an [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]] proved ineffective against Taivas's [[Magnet/Legends|magnetic]] fields, but fleets of the Alliance Remnant and the Empire-in-exile soon arrived to support their Jedi [[Alliance (Second Imperial Civil War)|allies]]. Krayt then unleashed his secret [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper|Sith trooper army]] against his opponents, and as the fanatical Sith [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] blasted through everyone who stood in their way, Geist was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by the [[Laser/Legends|guns]] of their [[Annihilator-class starfighter|''Annihilator''-class starfighters]]. ==Biography== ===A deal with devils=== {{Dialogue|Calixte|We ''wanted'' that war.|Fehlaaur'aitel'loro|But Roan Fel did not. And now ''we'' reap the consequences.|Geist|I was ''tired'' of Fel playing us against each other! It was time for the Moffs to reassert their authority like in the ''old'' days—back in the Remnant!|attr=The Moff Council reflects on the Sith–Imperial War|src=}} [[File:CouncilofMoffs-2006Legacy1.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Geist and the rest of the Moff Council swear fealty to Darth Krayt.]] Geist, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was the [[General/Legends|general]]''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' in [[Commander/Legends|command]] of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] of [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]]'s [[Fel Empire]] during the [[Sith–Imperial War]]. His position earned him the title of [[High Moff]] and a seat on the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]], a six-person group composed of three civilian and three [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] leaders. Geist's spot on the council in turn gave him control over a single [[Sector/Legends|sector]] as its [[Governor/Legends|governor]]. The Imperials' war against the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] began in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[127 ABY]],[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0]] largely due to the urgings of the [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] Council. Although Fel opposed the war, the council did not—Geist in particular felt the need for the Moffs to reassert their authority against Fel. However, the Empire soon found itself struggling against the [[Force power/Legends|abilities]] of the Alliance's allies in the [[New Jedi Order]]. [[128 ABY|One year into]] the war,[[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]] Moff [[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]] came to the rest of the council with an offer from the secretive [[One Sith]] of an alliance. That idea too was opposed by Fel, but the Moffs—each with his or her own agenda—were more receptive. The [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] thus joined the war effort on the Imperial side, and the Empire won victory after victory over the next two years.''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' In [[130 ABY]], the Empire [[Battle of Caamas|trapped and defeated]] the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet]] at the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Caamas/Legends|Caamas]], which prompted an Alliance surrender and the end of the war.[[Legacy (2006) 21|''Legacy'' (2006) 21]] As the Imperials took control of the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] world of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the Sith [[Massacre at Ossus|laid siege]] to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], [[Death/Legends|killing]] many of them and scattering the rest of the broken order throughout the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. On Coruscant, the Moff Council appeared before Fel in his new [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]], and [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Morlish Veed]] gave a report on the Jedi massacre. The meeting was interrupted by the arrival of [[Darth Krayt]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] who led the One Sith, and [[Darth Nihl|three]] [[Darth Maladi|of]] [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|his]] fellow [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]]. Krayt demanded Fel's life in exchange for the Sith's assistance in the war, and the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Attack on Emperor Fel|cut through]] Fel's [[Unidentified Imperial Knight 1|Imperial]] [[Unidentified Imperial Knight 2|Knight]] [[Unidentified short-haired Imperial Knight|bodyguards]] before slaying the Emperor himself. As Veed tried to prevent his fellow Moffs from interfering, Geist angered him by accusing him of having had advanced knowledge of the attack. Declaring himself the new [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]], Krayt commanded the Moffs to swear allegiance to him, which all six did before being dismissed. However, Krayt had in fact struck down a [[Roan Fel's double|double]] of Fel, allowing the real Fel to escape Coruscant and flee into [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]]. ===Servant of the Sith=== {{Quote|Doesn't matter now. Either the Sith have Skywalker, or he's dead. And there is ''nothing'' we can do about it! We have less real power than we did under Fel!|Geist, to the rest of the Moff Council|}} [[File:Geistslament.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Geist and Konrad Rus, during a meeting of the council]] [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] ruled much of the galaxy for the next seven years, while Fel established an [[Empire-in-exile]] and waged the [[Second Imperial Civil War]] against Krayt's forces.[[Legacy (2006) 2|''Legacy'' (2006) 2]] In [[137 ABY]], the [[Predator-class fighter|''Predator''-class fighters]] of Krayt's Empire's [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|Skull Squadron]] chased a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] through the [[Atmosphere/Legends|skies]] of Coruscant that matched a description of the [[Mynock (Helot-class)|''Mynock'']], the personal [[Helot-class medium space transport|craft]] of [[Cade Skywalker]].[[Legacy (2006) 14|''Legacy'' (2006) 14]] Skywalker was a former Jedi whose [[Dark transfer|powerful healing abilities]] Krayt sought, and in the midst of the stir caused by his ship's appearance, Geist and the rest of the Moff Council convened in their Coruscant [[Moff High Council meeting chambers|meeting chambers]]. The Sith Lady [[Darth Maladi]] joined them via [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] and told them not to waste any resources on [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] for Skywalker, refusing to give them any more details before signing off. Geist was the first of several Moffs to vocally lament the council's status as little more than puppets of the Sith. After Moff [[Konrad Rus]], the only council member present via hologram, signed off and departed the meeting, Grand [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Veed told the rest of the council that they needed to learn more about Skywalker, but Geist dismissed the idea as futile due to the Moffs' lack of real power. Veed broke up an ensuing argument between the Moffs and demanded that Skywalker be found, which brought the meeting to a close. Skywalker had in fact been [[Duel in the Temple of the Sith|captured]] by the Sith, and he spent [[Standard week/Legends|weeks]] as their [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoner]][[Legacy (2006) 17|''Legacy'' (2006) 17]] before being [[Skirmish at the Temple of the Sith|rescued]] by a group of his friends.[[Legacy (2006) 19|''Legacy'' (2006) 19]] ===Attack on Dac=== {{Dialogue|Grail|First wave of their evacuation fleet is beginning their run.|Stryfe|Target those ships. Bring our fleet to intercept.|Geist|You can't be serious! Stazi has to be the primary target! He's the primary threat! He just ''proved'' it!|Stryfe|Targeting the refugees will force Stazi to insert his own fleet between us and them. Fighters will cripple the evacuation while we grind Stazi to space debris, thus accomplishing ''both'' our goals. Do not distract me with your groundless objections again.|attr=An argument proceeds on the bridge of the ''Relentless'' during the evacuation of Dac|src=[[Legacy (2006) 47|''Legacy'' (2006) 47]]}} Later in the year, Krayt was betrayed and nearly killed by the Sith Lord [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Wyyrlok during a [[Battle on Had Abbadon|battle]] on the planet [[Had Abbadon]].[[Legacy (2006) 31|''Legacy'' (2006) 31]] Believing that his [[Sith Master/Legends|Master]] was indeed dead, Wyyrlok assumed control of the Empire and made Grand Admiral Veed his [[Regent/Legends|regent]], all the while maintaining the illusion that Krayt was merely resting in [[Suspended animation/Legends|stasis]].[[Legacy (2006) 38|''Legacy'' (2006) 38]] When Veed received intelligence that Roan Fel was planning to [[Mission to Agamar|negotiate]] an alliance with representatives of the recovering Jedi Order on the planet [[Agamar/Legends|Agamar]], he called a meeting of the Moff Council. Geist and the others convened in their chambers, where Veed outlined his plan: Moff [[Rulf Yage]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] would lead an assault on Agamar and attempt to kill or capture Fel.[[Legacy (2006) 43|''Legacy'' (2006) 43]] Geist did not participate in what Veed dubbed [[Operation: Thunderstroke]], and was instead sent to the planet [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. [[File:Stayouttastryfesway.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Darth Stryfe wins an argument with Geist during the evacuation of Dac.]] Krayt had earlier instigated the [[Genocide on Dac|genocide]] of Dac's native [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], and, around the same time as Operation: Thunderstroke, Wyyrlok ordered the Sith [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Vul Isen]] to accelerate the process by infecting the [[Water/Legends|water]] world's [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] with [[viral spore]]s. Dubbed the [[Final Protocol]], the [[Poison/Legends|poisoning]] would kill every living thing on the planet within a week. Wyyrlok anticipated an evacuation effort on the part of the [[Galactic Alliance Remnant]], a military organization that had recently allied with Fel,[[Legacy (2006) 36|''Legacy'' (2006) 36]] and he placed Geist in command of an operation to confront the [[Core Fleet|Galactic Alliance Core Fleet]] in the [[Realspace/Legends|space]] above Dac. Along with the Sith Lord [[Darth Stryfe]], who was overseeing the mission, Geist boarded Admiral [[Krion Grail]]'s [[Relentless (Pellaeon-class)|''Relentless'']], a [[Pellaeon-class Star Destroyer|''Pellaeon''-class Star Destroyer]] that served as the [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] of the [[Outer Rim Third Fleet]]. Ten [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]] into the Remnant's [[Evacuation of Dac|evacuation]] of the planet, Geist's strike force arrived at Dac. On the ''Relentless''{{'s}} [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]], Geist asserted that the Third [[Fleet/Legends|Fleet's]] primary target should be Remnant leader Admiral [[Gar Stazi]] and his flagship, the [[Alliance (Imperious-class)|''Alliance'']], but Stryfe was insistent that Darth Krayt's main goal was the extinction of the Mon Calamari. Stazi sent five ships vectoring toward the ''Relentless'', which Grail identified as [[Drone ship|drone vessels]] carrying bombs, and Geist ordered them destroyed. However, the ships contained [[Ion bomb/Legends|ion bombs]], which detonated and took down all of the Third Fleets' ships' [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]]. Grail ordered that available power be transferred to the ''Relentless''{{'s}} [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] just before the Remnant's [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] began a run through the Third Fleet's lines with [[Turbolaser/Legends|guns]] blazing. Five Imperial ships were destroyed or rendered derelict, but the Remnant fell back to protect their [[emergency evac fleet]] from retaliation. When the ''Relentless''{{'s}} sensors came back online, Stryfe demanded that they target the civilian evacuation vessels, and Geist's insistence that Stazi's effective tactics rendered him the primary target prompted Stryfe to lift him into the air by the neck. Stryfe argued that Stazi would use his own capital ships to shield the civilians, which the Admiral did after the Third Fleet destroyed several of the civilian ships. The Remnant fleet took heavy damage, but a fleet of the Empire-in-exile arrived from [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] and began to fire on the Sith–Imperial ships. Trapped between two foes, Geist was adamant that they retreat, and managed to convince a fanatical Stryfe that attempting to exterminate the Mon Calamari at the cost of their own lives would serve only to bring joy to their enemies. The Third Fleet thus jumped to [[Lightspeed/Legends|lightspeed]], and the evacuation of Dac continued unabated. Meanwhile, Roan Fel escaped from Agamar with his life.[[Legacy (2006) 46|''Legacy'' (2006) 46]] ===Into the fire=== {{Quote|Veed! By the Emperor's black bones, whose side are these ships on?! We're taking fire from them!''"
"''They're ''Krayt's!'' Pull your troops back! Get them out of the way! Let Krayt's troops through! Geist?! ''Geist?!|Geist and Morlish Veed, as the former is killed by Darth Krayt's Sith troopers|}} [[File:Leadingfromthefront.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Geist leads his troops against the Jedi's Hidden Temple.]] In [[138 ABY]], a fully-recovered Darth Krayt returned to Coruscant to [[Assault on the Temple of the Sith|slay Wyyrlok]], reclaim his throne,[[Legacy — War 1|''Legacy — War'' 1]] and launch a renewed military push against the Empire's enemies.[[Legacy — War 2|''Legacy — War'' 2]] Veed was dismissed from his position as regent, and the Moff Councils' numbers were thinned when Konrad Rus went missing and when Nyna Calixte fled Coruscant after Veed discovered that she was a [[Spy/Legends|spy]] for Roan Fel. When [[Antares Draco]], a captured [[Imperial Knight]] loyal to Fel, was [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] into revealing that the Jedi had a [[Hidden Temple (Sith–Imperial War)|Hidden Temple]] on the planet [[Taivas]], Krayt gathered Moffs Geist, Veed, and [[Fehlaaur'aitel'loro]] into his [[Darth Krayt's War Room|war room]] in the [[Temple of the Sith]]. He outlined his attack plan: Moff Yage would lead a [[Navy/Legends|naval]] [[Space warfare/Legends|assault]] on Taivas while Geist commanded [[Trooper/Legends|troops]] [[Ground warfare/Legends|on the ground]], and Veed would be in charge of their combined forces.[[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]] The Moffs departed the war room to prepare, unaware that they were merely bait in the Dark Lord's secret plan to destroy the [[Alliance (Second Imperial Civil War)|Alliance]] that had been formed between the Jedi, Empire-in-exile, and Galactic Alliance Remnant. The Imperial Navy soon afterward arrived at Taivas and began the [[Attack on the Hidden Temple]] with an [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|orbital bombardment]]. When high cliffs and powerful [[Magnet/Legends|magnetic]] fields prevented them from locking onto their target, Geist and his ground [[Troop/Legends|troops]] were sent in. Commanding [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], Imperial [[Walker/Legends|walkers]], TIE ''Predator''s, [[Sith-Imperial starfighter]]s, and a number of Sith [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]], Geist led his forces from the front lines and marched on foot with them toward their target: the Hidden Temple, which consisted of several starships turned upright in a pool of water. The Imperials fired on their targets, with a minimal defense coming only from the ''Mynock'' and a group of Jedi and Imperial Knights. A number of Sith fell to the defenders' [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], as did several stormtroopers and at least one walker, but the invasion force marched on largely unopposed. Meanwhile, in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], the Jedi sprang a trap when fleets of the Empire-in-exile and Galactic Alliance Remnant emerged from hyperspace and surrounded the Sith–Imperials. Krayt, however, had anticipated their arrival, and he sprang his own trap: the [[Dragon ship]]s and [[Annihilator-class starfighter|''Annihilator''-class]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] of his secret [[Darth Krayt's Sith trooper|Sith trooper army]] arrived at Taivas and were unleashed upon the gathered Alliance forces. Unflinchingly loyal to their Dark Lord, the Sith troopers blasted through friendly and enemy forces alike, and a group of them approached Taivas's surface. When they fired on the Imperial [[Army/Legends|Army]] forces that stood in their way, Geist frantically [[Comlink/Legends|commed]] Veed, who screamed at him to fall back. It was too late, however; an explosion caused by the ''Annihilator''s' [[Laser/Legends|guns]] killed Geist and most of his forces as the Sith troopers raced toward a confrontation with the Imperial Knights and Jedi. Outmatched by the new arrivals, the Alliance forces were only able to escape from the battle when a [[beam of light]] created by the death of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[T'ra Saa/Legends|T'ra Saa]] destroyed a number of Sith–Imperial ships and created an escape corridor. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Never had any use for Rus ''or'' his Imperial Mission. 'Victory Without War.' What's the ''point'' of ''that?|Geist|}} [[File:LegacyImps.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Geist accuses Morlish Veed of complicity with the Sith.]] Geist was the commanding general of the Imperial Army in the Fel Empire and later in Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire. His preferred tactics were threats and intimidation, and he had little regard for Konrad Rus's [[Imperial Mission]] and its "[[Victory Without War]]" mantra. Geist fought alongside his troops on the battlefield at Taivas, leading them on foot from the front lines. He had been eager for a chance to confront the Jedi for some time. As a member of the Moff Council in Krayt's Empire, Geist had a personal agenda. Like the rest of the council, he did not entirely trust any of his fellows, believing Rus to be a spy for Roan Fel and accusing Morlish Veed of having helped plan Krayt's usurping of Fel's throne. Despite being divided on a number of issues, the council limited their squabbling due to a fear of the power of the Sith. Geist despised the fact that the Moffs had little power in a Sith-controlled Empire, feeling as if they were a pack of [[Dog/Legends|dogs]] that the Sith could send to their kennels. He initially agreed to wage war on the Galactic Alliance and to form a partnership with the Sith after growing tired of Roan Fel playing the Moffs against one another, but he eventually came to realize that Fel's Empire had given him more power than Krayt's did. Geist idealized the [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]], which he saw as having granted its Moffs considerable authority. Although he was the leader of the Imperial Army, Geist was selected to command a naval mission to Dac. He dismissed Darth Stryfe's order to target the Mon Calamari, believing them to be insignificant "fish-heads," and openly argued with Stryfe on the ''Relentless''{{'s}} bridge. Geist thought that Gar Stazi and the Galactic Alliance Remnant were a significant threat to the Empire, but he chose to flee the battlefield when the odds pointed to his fleet's destruction. He was able to convince Darth Stryfe, who was dogmatically following his Master's wishes, to agree to the retreat. Geist had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], dark skin, and golden [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. ==Behind the scenes== Geist was created by [[John Ostrander]] and [[Jan Duursema]] as one of the villains in the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic series]], with his first appearance coming in ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 1|inaugural issue]], released in [[June]] [[2006]]. He made sporadic appearances throughout the rest of the series before being killed in the [[Legacy — War 4|fourth issue]] of its followup miniseries, [[Star Wars: Legacy — War|''Star Wars: Legacy—War'']], released in [[March]] [[2011]]. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Geist}} *[[Legacy (2006) 1|''Legacy'' (2006) 1]] {{1st}} *[[Legacy (2006) 8|''Legacy'' (2006) 8]] *[[Legacy (2006) 15|''Legacy'' (2006) 15]] *[[Legacy (2006) 43|''Legacy'' (2006) 43]] *[[Legacy (2006) 47|''Legacy'' (2006) 47]] *[[Legacy — War 3|''Legacy — War'' 3]] *[[Legacy — War 4|''Legacy — War'' 4]] ==Sources== *{{InsiderCite|89|The Art of War (Star Wars Insider 89)}} {{Po}} *[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]] *{{CSWECite|II||Imperial Remnant}} {{Po}} *''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Moffs130ABY}} {{Interlang |de=Geist (Moff) |es=Geist |pl=Geist }} [[Category:Admirals and generals of Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Fel Empire military personnel]] [[Category:High Moffs]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Army generals]] [[Category:Imperial Army officers of Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Moff Council members]] [[Category:Moffs of Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Moffs of the Fel Empire]]
